autoresearch_log.baseline_note: DOM-based QA scored 100%, but visual inspection of Puppeteer screenshots revealed Chart.js X-axis labels rendering literal '\n' instead of line breaks
autoresearch_log.discovery_method: Manual screenshot review of all 15 slides (3 decks × 5 slides)

change.before: JSON.stringify(data.left_labels) — passes literal '1Q\nFY24' string to Chart.js
change.after: chartLabelsToJs() — splits '\n' labels into arrays ['1Q','FY24'] for Chart.js multi-line rendering

autoresearch_log.reasoning: Chart.js renders string labels as-is but interprets array labels as multi-line. The build script was passing '1Q\nFY24' as a single string, which rendered the literal backslash-n. New chartLabelsToJs() helper splits on \n to produce arrays.

side_effects.added: C5_no_literal_escape check to checklist.json (detects literal escape sequences in DOM text — note: cannot detect canvas-rendered text)
